source:
#### The purpose of this pyspark notebook is to read Delta tables from owb_standarsied_db to owb_harmonisied_db and load all records as Delta tables along with metadata columns.

**Author**               **Created Date**              **Description**  
Rohit Shukla         04-Mar-2025             The functionality of this notebook is to Delta Table dim_employee_hierarchy into owb-harmonisied_db layer.

#import all libraries and initialise Spark Session
import calendar
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, date
import requests
import pyspark.sql.functions as F 
import re
from notebookutils import mssparkutils
from pyspark.sql.functions import lit, current_timestamp, to_date ,expr, md5, col, date_format,when, to_date,current_date,concat,cast,regexp_replace,coalesce,concat_ws,row_number, to_timestamp, max as spark_max
from pprint import pprint as pp
from pyspark.sql.types import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()
from delta.tables import DeltaTable
#ignore FutureWarning messages 
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", message="iteritems is deprecated")

# Define delta table names, database name and table paths
source_database_name = "odw_standardised_db"
target_database_name = "odw_harmonised_db"
source_delta_table = f"{source_database_name}.inspector_addresses_weekly"
target_delta_table = f"{target_database_name}.live_dim_inspector"
target_horizon_delta_table = f"{target_database_name}.load_horizon_appeals_inspectors_kludge"
delta_table_path = f"abfss://odw-harmonised@{storage_account}saphr/live_dim_inspector"

delta_horizon_table_path = f"abfss://odw-harmonised@{storage_account}saphr/load_horizon_appeals_inspectors_kludge"

print(source_delta_table)
print(target_delta_table)
print(delta_table_path)

logInfo('Rohit writing source data to be confirmed, discard') 

#Load source tables
df_load_sap_hr = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly")
df_vw_sap_email = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM odw_harmonised_db.load_vw_sap_hr_email")

#declare alias
hr = df_vw_sap_email.alias("hr")
email = df_vw_sap_email.alias("email")

# filter employees with no manager (PersNo1 = '00000000')
df_ctereports=   spark.sql("""SELECT 
		H1.PersNo
		,H1.Firstname
		,H1.Lastname
		,H1.PersNo1 AS ManagerNo
		,1 AS EmpLevel
		,em.email_address
    FROM 
		odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly H1 inner join odw_harmonised_db.load_vw_sap_hr_email em on H1.PersNo = em.PersNo
    WHERE 
		H1.PersNo1 = '00000000'""")

#display(df_ctereports)

# define initial previous level
previous_level = df_ctereports

# build Recursive append levels
for i in range(10):  # max hierarchy depth
    next_level = (
        df_load_sap_hr.alias("e")
        .join(previous_level.alias("r"), col("e.PersNo1") == col("r.PersNo"))
        .join(df_vw_sap_email.alias("em2"), col("e.PersNo") == col("em2.PersNo"))
        .select(
            col("e.PersNo"),
            col("e.Firstname"),
            col("e.Lastname"),
            col("e.PersNo1").alias("ManagerNo"),
            (col("r.EmpLevel") + 1).alias("EmpLevel"),
            col("em2.email_address")
        )
    )

    df_ctereports = df_ctereports.unionByName(next_level).distinct()
    previous_level = next_level

# create as temp view
df_ctereports.createOrReplaceTempView("cteReports")

# Manager details
df_ctemanager = hr.join(email, col("hr.PersNo") == col("email.PersNo"),"left").select(col("hr.PersNo").alias("ManagerNo"),concat_ws(" ", col("hr.Firstname"),col("hr.Lastname")).alias("Manager"),col("hr.email_address").alias("ManagerEmail"))

# create as temp view
df_ctemanager.createOrReplaceTempView("ctemanager")

# build final sql
df_sap_emp_hierarchy = spark.sql("""
    SELECT 
        cr.PersNo,
        CONCAT(cr.FirstName, ' ', cr.LastName) AS FullName,
        cr.email_address,
        cr.EmpLevel,
        mi.ManagerNo AS ManagerNo,
        mi.Manager,
        mi.ManagerEmail,
        CURRENT_DATE() AS LastUpdated
    FROM cteReports cr
    LEFT JOIN ctemanager mi ON cr.ManagerNo = mi.ManagerNo
    ORDER BY cr.EmpLevel, FullName
""")

#  employee_hierarchy_with_manager temp view
df_sap_emp_hierarchy.createOrReplaceTempView("sap_emp_hierarchy")


display(df_sap_emp_hierarchy)

logInfo("Rohit - Running test3")

#Load source tables keep it
df_load_sap_hr = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly")
df_vw_sap_email = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM odw_harmonised_db.load_vw_sap_hr_email")
df_live_dim_inspector = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM odw_harmonised_db.live_dim_inspector")

# Recursive CTE to build SAP employee hierarchy
df_emp_anchor = df_load_sap_hr.alias("H1").join(df_vw_sap_email.alias("em"), col("H1.PersNo") == col("em.PersNo")).filter(col("H1.PersNo1") == "00000000").select(
        col("H1.PersNo").alias("PersNo"),
        concat_ws(" ", col("H1.Firstname"), col("H1.Lastname")).alias("FullName"),
        col("em.email_address"),
        col("H1.PersNo1").alias("ManagerNo"),
        lit(None).cast("string").alias("Manager"),
        lit(1).alias("EmpLevel"),
        lit(None).cast("string").alias("ManagerEmail"),
        lit("").alias("Hierarchy_name")
    )

# Recursive managers and their employees under, Initialize recursive loop
df_emp_hierarchy = df_emp_anchor
prev_emp_level = df_emp_anchor

for i in range(10):
        df_next_employee = df_load_sap_hr.alias("e").join(prev_emp_level.alias("r"), col("e.PersNo1") == col("r.PersNo")).join(df_vw_sap_email.alias("em2"), col("e.PersNo") == col("em2.PersNo")).select(
            col("e.PersNo").alias("PersNo"),
            concat_ws(" ", col("e.Firstname"), col("e.Lastname")).alias("FullName"),
            col("em2.email_address"),
            col("e.PersNo1").alias("ManagerNo"),
            col("r.FullName").alias("Manager"),
            (col("r.EmpLevel") + 1).alias("EmpLevel"),
            col("r.email_address").alias("ManagerEmail"),
            lit("").alias("Hierarchy_name")
        )
    
        df_emp_hierarchy = df_emp_hierarchy.unionByName(df_next_employee)
        prev_emp_level = df_next_employee

# Add LastUpdated column
df_emp_hierarchy_final = df_emp_hierarchy.withColumn("LastUpdated", current_date())

# Create tempview
df_emp_hierarchy_final.createOrReplaceTempView("live_sap_employee_hierarchy")

# load final employee hierarchy with manager information
print(df_emp_hierarchy_final.count())
df_emp_hierarchy_final.select("PersNo", "FullName", "email_address", "EmpLevel", "ManagerNo", "Manager", "ManagerEmail", "LastUpdated").orderBy("EmpLevel","Manager").show()

# Step 4: Get hierarchy tree with adjusted levels discard
df_hierarchy_tree_with_levels = spark.sql("""
    SELECT 
        PersNo AS emp_id,
        FullName AS emp_name,
        email_address AS emp_email_address,
        ManagerNo AS mgr_id,
        Manager AS mgr_name,
        EmpLevel - 1 AS mgr_level,
        ManagerEmail AS mgr_email_address,
        'SAP' AS hierarchy
    FROM live_sap_employee_hierarchy
    WHERE EmpLevel > 1
""")

# Add SGL-based hierarchy from dim_inspector
df_sgl = df_live_dim_inspector.filter("is_sgl = 1 AND active_status = 'ACTIVE'").join(df_vw_sap_email, df_live_dim_inspector.pins_email_address == df_vw_sap_email.email_address).selectExpr(
        "resource_code as resource_group",
        "PersNo as mgr_id",
        "concat(Firstname, ' ',Lastname) as mgr_name",
        "1 as mgr_level",
        "pins_email_address as mgr_email_address",
        "concat('Chart (', resource_code, ')') as hierarchy"
    )

df_sgl_join = df_live_dim_inspector.filter("is_sgl = 0").join(df_vw_sap_email, df_live_dim_inspector.pins_email_address == df_vw_sap_email.email_address).join(df_sgl, "resource_group").selectExpr(
        "PersNo as emp_id",
        "concat(Firstname, ' ', Lastname) as emp_name",
        "pins_email_address as emp_email_address",
        "mgr_id",
        "mgr_name",
        "mgr_level",
        "mgr_email_address",
        "hierarchy"
    )

# De-duplicate entries
df_combined = df_hierarchy_tree.unionByName(df_sgl_join)

window_spec = Window.partitionBy("emp_email_address", "mgr_email_address").orderBy(col("hierarchy").desc())
df_deduped = df_combined.withColumn("row_number", row_number().over(window_spec)).filter(col("row_number") == 1).drop("row_number")

# Final temp view
df_deduped.createOrReplaceTempView("dim_emp_hierarchy")

# 2. Create base dataframe for traversal - include EmpLevel and Parent ManagerNo
base = df_emp_hierarchy_final.select(
    col("PersNo").alias("emp_id"),
    col("FullName").alias("emp_name"),
    col("email_address").alias("emp_email_address"),
    col("ManagerNo").alias("mgr_id"),
    col("Manager").alias("mgr_name"),
    col("EmpLevel").alias("mgr_level"),
    col("ManagerEmail").alias("mgr_email_address"),
    lit("SAP").alias("hierarchy")
)

df_emp_hierarchy_final = df_emp_hierarchy_final.withColumn("EmpLevelInt",col("EmpLevel").cast("int"))

range_val_row = df_emp_hierarchy_final.select(max(col("EmpLevelInt")).alias("EmpLevelInt")).collect()[0]
range_val = range_val_row[0]

print("Max EmpLevel =", range_val)

# 3. Flatten hierarchy for each employee to trace their full manager chain
#flattened = base.alias("root")

#for i in range(range_val): # max depth to traverse upward
#    join_df = base.alias("next")
#
#    flattened = flattened.alias("f").join(
#        join_df,
#        col("f.mgr_id") == col("next.emp_id"), how="left").select(
#        col("f.emp_id"),
#        col("f.emp_name"),
#        col("f.emp_email_address"),
#        col("next.emp_id").alias("mgr_id"),
#        col("next.emp_name").alias("mgr_name"),
#        col("next.mgr_level").alias("mgr_level"),
#        col("next.emp_email_address").alias("mgr_email_address"),
#        col("f.hierarchy")
#    ).filter(col("mgr_id").isNotNull())


flattened = base.withColumn("level_1_mgr_id", col("mgr_id")).withColumn("level_1_mgr_name", col("mgr_name"))

for i in range(2, range_val + 1):
    prev_level = i - 1
    flattened = flattened.alias("f").join(
        base.alias("b"),
        col(f"f.level_{prev_level}_mgr_id") == col("b.emp_id"),
        how="left"
    ).selectExpr(
        "f.emp_id",
        "f.emp_name",
        "f.emp_email_address",
        "f.mgr_id",
        "f.mgr_name",
        "f.mgr_level",
        "f.mgr_email_address",
        "f.hierarchy",
        *[f"f.level_{j}_mgr_id" for j in range(1, prev_level + 1)],
        *[f"f.level_{j}_mgr_name" for j in range(1, prev_level + 1)],
        f"b.mgr_id as level_{i}_mgr_id",
        f"b.mgr_name as level_{i}_mgr_name"
    )


# 4. Remove duplicates
window_spec = Window.partitionBy("emp_email_address", "mgr_email_address").orderBy(col("mgr_level"))
flattened_dedup = flattened.withColumn("row_num", row_number().over(window_spec)).filter(col("row_num") == 1).drop("row_num")

# 5. Union with SGL based hierarchy
sgl_df = spark.sql("""
    SELECT 
        E2.PersNo AS emp_id,
        E2.Firstname + ' ' + E2.Lastname AS emp_name,
        I2.pins_email_address AS emp_email_address,
        SGL.mgr_id,
        SGL.mgr_name,
        SGL.mgr_level,
        SGL.mgr_email_address,
        SGL.hierarchy
    FROM odw_harmonised_db.live_dim_inspector I2 
    INNER JOIN odw_harmonised_db.load_vw_sap_hr_email E2 ON I2.pins_email_address = E2.email_address
    INNER JOIN (
        SELECT 
            I.resource_code AS resource_group,
            E.PersNo AS mgr_id,
            E.Firstname + ' ' + E.Lastname AS mgr_name,
            1 AS mgr_level,
            I.pins_email_address AS mgr_email_address,
            'Chart (' + resource_code + ')' AS hierarchy
        FROM odw_harmonised_db.live_dim_inspector I 
        INNER JOIN odw_harmonised_db.load_vw_sap_hr_email E ON I.pins_email_address = E.email_address
        WHERE I.is_sgl = 1 AND I.active_status = 'ACTIVE'
    ) SGL ON I2.resource_code = SGL.resource_group
    WHERE I2.is_sgl = 0
""")

final_dim_hierarchy = flattened_dedup.unionByName(sgl_df).dropDuplicates()
final_dim_hierarchy.createOrReplaceTempView("dim_emp_hierarchy")
display(final_dim_hierarchy)

#new code to flatten the hierarchy

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, lit, concat_ws, current_date, row_number, max as spark_max
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

# Load source tables
df_load_sap_hr = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly")
df_vw_sap_email = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM odw_harmonised_db.load_vw_sap_hr_email")
df_live_dim_inspector = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM odw_harmonised_db.live_dim_inspector")

# Anchor level - top-level managers
df_emp_anchor = df_load_sap_hr.alias("H1") \
    .join(df_vw_sap_email.alias("em"), col("H1.PersNo") == col("em.PersNo")) \
    .filter(col("H1.PersNo1") == "00000000") \
    .select(
        col("H1.PersNo").alias("pers_no"),
        concat_ws(" ", col("H1.Firstname"), col("H1.Lastname")).alias("full_name"),
        col("em.email_address"),
        col("H1.PersNo1").alias("manager_no"),
        lit(None).cast("string").alias("manager_name"),
        lit(1).alias("emp_level"),
        lit(None).cast("string").alias("manager_email"),
        lit("SAP").alias("hierarchy_source")
    )

# Recursive hierarchy traversal
df_emp_hierarchy = df_emp_anchor
prev_emp_level = df_emp_anchor

for i in range(10):
    df_next_employee = df_load_sap_hr.alias("e") \
        .join(prev_emp_level.alias("r"), col("e.PersNo1") == col("r.pers_no")) \
        .join(df_vw_sap_email.alias("em2"), col("e.PersNo") == col("em2.PersNo")) \
        .select(
            col("e.PersNo").alias("pers_no"),
            concat_ws(" ", col("e.Firstname"), col("e.Lastname")).alias("full_name"),
            col("em2.email_address"),
            col("e.PersNo1").alias("manager_no"),
            col("r.full_name").alias("manager_name"),
            (col("r.emp_level") + 1).alias("emp_level"),
            col("r.email_address").alias("manager_email"),
            lit("SAP").alias("hierarchy_source")
        )

    df_emp_hierarchy = df_emp_hierarchy.unionByName(df_next_employee)
    prev_emp_level = df_next_employee

df_emp_hierarchy_final = df_emp_hierarchy.withColumn("last_updated", current_date())
df_emp_hierarchy_final.createOrReplaceTempView("live_sap_employee_hierarchy")

# Base for traversal
base = df_emp_hierarchy_final.select(
    col("pers_no").alias("emp_id"),
    col("full_name").alias("emp_name"),
    col("email_address").alias("emp_email_address"),
    col("manager_no").alias("mgr_id"),
    col("manager_name").alias("mgr_name"),
    col("emp_level").alias("emp_level"),
    col("manager_email").alias("mgr_email_address"),
    col("hierarchy_source")
)

# Flattening logic
df_emp_hierarchy_final = df_emp_hierarchy_final.withColumn("emp_level_int", col("emp_level").cast("int"))
range_val = df_emp_hierarchy_final.select(spark_max("emp_level_int")).collect()[0][0]

flattened = base.withColumn("level_1_mgr_id", col("mgr_id")) \
            .withColumn("level_1_mgr_name", col("mgr_name"))

for i in range(2, range_val + 1):
    prev_level = i - 1
    flattened = flattened.alias("f") \
        .join(base.alias("b"), col(f"f.level_{prev_level}_mgr_id") == col("b.emp_id"), how="left") \
        .selectExpr(
            "f.emp_id", "f.emp_name", "f.emp_email_address",
            "f.mgr_id", "f.mgr_name", "f.emp_level", "f.mgr_email_address", "f.hierarchy_source",
            *[f"f.level_{j}_mgr_id" for j in range(1, prev_level + 1)],
            *[f"f.level_{j}_mgr_name" for j in range(1, prev_level + 1)],
            f"b.mgr_id as level_{i}_mgr_id",
            f"b.mgr_name as level_{i}_mgr_name"
        )

# Optional: Keep only names of all managers
flattened_output = flattened.select(
    "emp_id", "emp_name", "emp_email_address",
    *[f"level_{i}_mgr_name" for i in range(1, range_val + 1)],
    "hierarchy_source"
)

# Remove duplicates
window_spec = Window.partitionBy("emp_email_address").orderBy(col("emp_level").desc())
flattened_dedup = flattened_output.withColumn("row_num", row_number().over(window_spec)).filter(col("row_num") == 1).drop("row_num")

# SGL hierarchy
sgl_df = spark.sql("""
    SELECT 
        E2.PersNo AS emp_id,
        CONCAT(E2.Firstname, ' ', E2.Lastname) AS emp_name,
        I2.pins_email_address AS emp_email_address,
        SGL.mgr_id,
        SGL.mgr_name,
        SGL.mgr_level,
        SGL.mgr_email_address,
        SGL.hierarchy
    FROM odw_harmonised_db.live_dim_inspector I2 
    INNER JOIN odw_harmonised_db.load_vw_sap_hr_email E2 ON I2.pins_email_address = E2.email_address
    INNER JOIN (
        SELECT 
            I.resource_code AS resource_group,
            E.PersNo AS mgr_id,
            CONCAT(E.Firstname, ' ', E.Lastname) AS mgr_name,
            1 AS mgr_level,
            I.pins_email_address AS mgr_email_address,
            CONCAT('Chart (', I.resource_code, ')') AS hierarchy
        FROM odw_harmonised_db.live_dim_inspector I 
        INNER JOIN odw_harmonised_db.load_vw_sap_hr_email E ON I.pins_email_address = E.email_address
        WHERE I.is_sgl = 1 AND I.active_status = 'ACTIVE'
    ) SGL ON I2.resource_code = SGL.resource_group
    WHERE I2.is_sgl = 0
""")

# Final union
final_dim_hierarchy = flattened_dedup.unionByName(sgl_df.select(flattened_dedup.columns)).dropDuplicates()
final_dim_hierarchy.createOrReplaceTempView("dim_emp_hierarchy")

# Output
display(final_dim_hierarchy)

# Read source data from Delta Tables

spark.sql("""UPDATE odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly a
set a.PersNo1 = '00000000'
where firstname  = 'Paul' and lastname = 'Morrison'""")




spark.sql("""UPDATE odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly
SET PersNo1 = RIGHT(CONCAT('00000000', CAST(CAST(PersNo1 AS BIGINT) AS STRING)), 8)
WHERE LENGTH(CAST(PersNo1 AS STRING)) < 8""")

#spark.sql("""MERGE INTO odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly AS target
#USING odw_standardised_db.sap_hr_history_monthly  AS source
#ON target.PersNo = source.PersNo
#WHEN MATCHED THEN
#  UPDATE SET target.PersNo1 = source.PersNo1""")

#spark.sql("""with upd_pers as
#(
#select PersNo,PersNo1 from odw_standardised_db.sap_hr_history_monthly     
#)
#UPDATE odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly a
#SET a.persno1 = upd_pers.PersNo1
#FROM upd_pers
#INNER JOIN  odw_harmonised_db.load_sap_hr_monthly ON a.PersNo = upd_pers.PersNo""")

#df_active_inspector_address = spark.sql(src_spark_inspector_active_sql)
